articles.description: A Roseburg man faces multiple charges after authorities say he fired at vehicles on Interstate 5 Saturday night before leading police into Winston, where deputies used a spike strip to stop him. One driver reported a bullet shattered his truck’s back window.
articles.title: Roseburg man accused of firing at vehicles on Interstate 5, leading deputies on pursuit into Winston

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police reported six incidents Monday, including a theft, camping violations, a parole violation, an illegal burn, and a traffic crash involving a suspended driver, according to the City of Roseburg Police Logs.
articles.title: Police Log: March 9, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop
articles.title: March 10, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: KQUA is expanding community radio across much of Douglas County with its new 90.5 FM signal. A public soft-launch celebration is set for March 12 in downtown Roseburg, where organizers hope listeners, volunteers and future hosts will help shape the station’s future.
articles.title: KQUA Expands Community Radio Reach Across The Umpqua Valley With New 90.5 FM Signal

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: CASA of Douglas County will host a free coffee meet-and-greet from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. Tuesday, March 10, at Highwater Cafe & Market in Reedsport, where community members can meet Executive Director Angela Gross and learn how to become a child advocate.
articles.title: CASA Of Douglas County To Host Coffee Meet-And-Greet In Reedsport

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police reported dozens of incidents from March 6-9, including DUII arrests, warrants, thefts, trespass cases, prohibited camping citations, crashes and disorderly conduct, according to the City of Roseburg Police Logs service.
articles.title: Police Log: March 6 - March 9, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: BLM timber sales in Oregon brought in more than $8.3 million from 27.6 million board feet sold across 1,255 acres. The Roseburg District’s Muley Mount tract accounted for 5 million board feet and sold to Scott Timber Company of Roseburg.
articles.title: BLM Timber Sales In Oregon Top $8.3 Million, With Roseburg Tract Included

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Safe Haven’s March Blessing Bag Giveaway is set for March 13-14 at the Meyer Activity Center in Roseburg. Free clothing, diapers, formula, baby equipment, maternity clothes, blankets and more will be available. Bring your own bags. Saturday continues while supplies last.
articles.title: Safe Haven Announces March Blessing Bag Giveaway In Roseburg

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: The Glide community honored 2025 First Citizens Bill DeGroot and Kara Weber on Saturday during the annual First Citizens luncheon, a tradition dating back to 1960 that celebrates service, leadership and the people who help shape the Glide community.
articles.title: Glide Honors 2025 First Citizens While Celebrating a Tradition Dating Back to 1960

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: The Glide community honored 2025 First Citizens Bill DeGroot and Kara Beber on Saturday during the annual First Citizens luncheon, a tradition dating back to 1950 that celebrates service, leadership and the people who help shape the Glide community.
articles.title: Glide Honors 2025 First Citizens While Celebrating a Tradition Dating Back to 1950

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: A 33-year-old Drain man died Friday afternoon after a single-vehicle crash near Yoncalla. Deputies said Nicholas Rex Snyder was found ejected from a white 2009 Kia Rio in a field off Elkhead Road after the crash was discovered by a FedEx driver.
articles.title: Drain Man Killed In Crash Near Yoncalla
